items.verseEn: Say, "I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak
And from the evil of darkness when it settles
And from the evil of the blowers in knots
And from the evil of an envier when he envies."

categories.name: جُمُوعُ تَكْسِيرٍ عَلَى وَزْنِ فُعُل
categories.nameEn: Broken plurals on the morphological pattern fuʿul (فُعُل)

items.verse: ۞ وَإِذَا رَأَيْتَهُمْ تُعْجِبُكَ أَجْسَامُهُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَقُولُوا۟ تَسْمَعْ لِقَوْلِهِمْ ۖ كَأَنَّهُمْ خُشُبٌۭ مُّسَنَّدَةٌۭ ۖ يَحْسَبُونَ كُلَّ صَيْحَةٍ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ هُمُ ٱلْعَدُوُّ فَٱحْذَرْهُمْ ۚ قَـٰتَلَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۖ أَنَّىٰ يُؤْفَكُونَ
items.verseEn: And when you see them, their forms please you, and if they speak, you listen to their speech. [They are] as if they were pieces of wood propped up - they think that every shout is against them. They are the enemy, so beware of them. May Allāh destroy them; how are they deluded?
Devoted [to their husbands] and of equal age,
As if they were alarmed donkeys.

categories.name: مُرَادِفَاتُ الْجَنَّةِ وَأَوْصَافُهَا
categories.nameEn: Synonyms or descriptive terms for Paradise and its gardens

items.verse: تَرَى ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ مُشْفِقِينَ مِمَّا كَسَبُوا۟ وَهُوَ وَاقِعٌۢ بِهِمْ ۗ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فِى رَوْضَاتِ ٱلْجَنَّاتِ ۖ لَهُم مَّا يَشَآءُونَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَضْلُ ٱلْكَبِيرُ
items.verseEn: You will see the wrongdoers fearful of what they have earned, and it will [certainly] befall them. And those who have believed and done righteous deeds will be in lush regions of the gardens [in Paradise] having whatever they will in the presence of their Lord. That is what is the great bounty.

items.verse: أَمَّنْ خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَأَنزَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءًۭ فَأَنۢبَتْنَا بِهِۦ حَدَآئِقَ ذَاتَ بَهْجَةٍۢ مَّا كَانَ لَكُمْ أَن تُنۢبِتُوا۟ شَجَرَهَآ ۗ أَءِلَـٰهٌۭ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌۭ يَعْدِلُونَ
items.verseEn: [More precisely], is He [not best] who created the heavens and the earth and sent down for you rain from the sky, causing to grow thereby gardens of joyful beauty which you could not [otherwise] have grown the trees thereof? Is there a deity with Allāh? [No], but they are a people who ascribe equals [to Him].

items.verse: جَنَّـٰتُ عَدْنٍۢ يَدْخُلُونَهَا وَمَن صَلَحَ مِنْ ءَابَآئِهِمْ وَأَزْوَٰجِهِمْ وَذُرِّيَّـٰتِهِمْ ۖ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ يَدْخُلُونَ عَلَيْهِم مِّن كُلِّ بَابٍۢ
items.verseEn: Gardens of perpetual residence; they will enter them with whoever were righteous among their forefathers, their spouses and their descendants. And the angels will enter upon them from every gate, [saying],

items.verse: فِى مَقْعَدِ صِدْقٍ عِندَ مَلِيكٍۢ مُّقْتَدِرٍۭ
items.verseEn: In a seat of honor near a Sovereign, Perfect in Ability.

categories.name: أَسْمَاءٌ وَقَعَتْ مُبْتَدَأً مُؤَخَّرًا خَبَرُهُ شِبْهُ جُمْلَةٍ
categories.nameEn: Nouns that are the delayed subject (mubtada' mu'akhkhar) of a nominal sentence whose predicate is a prepositional phrase (shibh jumlah)

items.verse: لَّا يَتَّخِذِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ أَوْلِيَآءَ مِن دُونِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۖ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ فَلَيْسَ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ فِى شَىْءٍ إِلَّآ أَن تَتَّقُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ تُقَىٰةًۭ ۗ وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ نَفْسَهُۥ ۗ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمَصِيرُ
items.verseEn: Let not believers take disbelievers as allies [i.e., supporters or protectors] rather than believers. And whoever [of you] does that has nothing [i.e., no association] with Allāh, except when taking precaution against them in prudence. And Allāh warns you of Himself, and to Allāh is the [final] destination.
To your Lord, that Day, is the [place of] permanence.

items.verse: وَلَهُ ٱلْجَوَارِ ٱلْمُنشَـَٔاتُ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ كَٱلْأَعْلَـٰمِ
items.verseEn: And to Him belong the ships [with sails] elevated in the sea like mountains.

items.verse: وَلَهُ ٱلْحَمْدُ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَعَشِيًّۭا وَحِينَ تُظْهِرُونَ
items.verseEn: And to Him is [due all] praise throughout the heavens and the earth. And [exalted is He] at night and when you are at noon.

harf.hint: Not a king, but the authority, proof, or power granted by God.
harf.arabicVerse: يَـٰمَعْشَرَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ إِنِ ٱسْتَطَعْتُمْ أَن تَنفُذُوا۟ مِنْ أَقْطَارِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱنفُذُوا۟ ۚ لَا تَنفُذُونَ إِلَّا بِسُلْطَـٰنٍۢ
harf.verse: 55:33 — O company of jinn and mankind, if you are able to pass beyond the regions of the heavens and the earth, then pass. You will not pass except by authority [from Allāh].

deduction.title: The Promised Heir
deduction.intro: In the quiet solitude of a sanctuary, a devout servant of Allah raised a prayer for an heir. An answer came, not as expected, but as a divine promise delivered by angels—a sign that would precede a life of wisdom and piety. This promised individual would confirm a Word from Allah, destined for honor and righteousness from the very beginning.
clues: I was a gift from Allah, an answer to a heartfelt prayer made in old age.
Wisdom was granted to me while I was still a child.
I was known for my kindness to my parents and was never arrogant or rebellious.
My purpose was to confirm a Word from Allah, a messenger who would come after me.
As a sign of my impending arrival, my father was rendered unable to speak to people for three days.
My name was unique, given by Allah Himself, a name no one had been called before me.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Ishaq
Prophet Yahya
Prophet 'Isa
Prophet Isma'il
Prophet Zakariyya

trial.label: What was my primary role?
options: Building the Kaaba
Leading an exodus from Egypt
Confirming the coming of Prophet 'Isa
Surviving inside a great fish
Interpreting the dreams of a king

location.label: What was the sign of my arrival?
options: A staff that turned into a serpent
A flood that covered the earth
A camel emerging from a rock
My father's temporary inability to speak
A flock of birds pelting an army with stones
location.answer: My father's temporary inability to speak

outcome.label: How was I described?
options: Given dominion over the winds and jinn
Noble, chaste, and a righteous prophet
Able to mold iron and given a beautiful voice
Thrown into a well by my own brothers
The first to build an ark
outcome.answer: Noble, chaste, and a righteous prophet

deduction.arabic: فَنَادَتْهُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ وَهُوَ قَآئِمٌۭ يُصَلِّى فِى ٱلْمِحْرَابِ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُبَشِّرُكَ بِيَحْيَىٰ مُصَدِّقًۢا بِكَلِمَةٍۢ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَسَيِّدًۭا وَحَصُورًۭا وَنَبِيًّۭا مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ
deduction.verse: So the angels called him while he was standing in prayer in the chamber, "Indeed, Allāh gives you good tidings of John, confirming a word from Allāh and [who will be] honorable, abstaining [from women], and a prophet from among the righteous." (3:39)

translations: And He has subjected for you the night
and the day and the sun and the moon
and the stars (are) subjected by His command
surely (are) signs for a people who use reason
scramble.hint: This verse describes how celestial bodies are made subservient by Allah's command as signs for thinking people.
scramble.arabic: وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ وَٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ ۖ وَٱلنُّجُومُ مُسَخَّرَٰتٌۢ بِأَمْرِهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍۢ لِّقَوْمٍۢ يَعْقِلُونَ
scramble.verseEn: And He has subjected for you the night and day and the sun and moon, and the stars are subjected by His command. Indeed in that are signs for a people who reason.
